methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Young workers feeling AI pressure without evidence of deep capability-building suggests shallow anxiety rather than distributed empowerment—concentration of disruption without corresponding skill distribution.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI trainer emphasizing worker leadership over technology signals potential for deep, distributed capability if training reaches beyond metros—critical test of whether peer networks can form in traditionally underserved regions.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: Community-driven workforce training backed by EPA grants in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capacity-building outside traditional tech hubs—exactly the distributed, deep development that distinguishes Build It Yourself from concentrated excellence.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU-AI Ready RVA partnership concentrates AI education infrastructure in Richmond Metro, deepening capability but raising questions about whether this model transfers beyond university-adjacent communities to rural Virginia.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: MLS introducing AI tool for home searches exemplifies platform-delivered adoption in Hampton Roads—broad but shallow usage that distributes AI benefits without building local capability to develop such systems.
